query: effect of surgeon experience on transurethral resection of the prostate

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the indications for performing transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP). Describe the equipment, personnel, preparation, and technique needed for transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP). Review the appropriate evaluation of the potential complications and their clinical significance with transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP). Outline some interprofessional team strategies for improving care coordination and communication to advance transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP) and improve outcomes. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Collaboration, shared decision-making, and communication are critical elements for a good outcome. The interprofessional care provided to the patient must use an integrated care pathway combined with an evidence-based approach to planning and evaluating all joint activities. The earlier the signs and symptoms of a complication are identified, the better the prognosis and outcome of the procedure. [Level 4]

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: TURP is typically an elective procedure used to treat prostatic enlargement resulting in symptomatic bladder outlet obstruction. Because of this, appropriate indications and discussion of the complications associated with the procedure require an integrated interprofessional team to communicate effectively and initiate treatment plans so the patient and family have appropriate expectations. This approach will ensure that patients are adequately prepared for their procedure and what to expect. Patients who are better informed are more invested in their decision and are happier with their outcomes. The procedure is elective and irreversible, so some extra review and discussion with the patient and family are reasonable.

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- Pearls: Summary of Tips for Performing Large Prostates by TURP
retrieved_docs.content: Do not attempt a larger prostate than you can reasonably handle within 90 minutes of operating time. Have everything ready and prepared, so you don't have to stop for supplies, instruments, or catheters. Be prepared to stop after just completing one side if it's taking too long. It is always possible to return another day to finish if necessary. Establish a regular rhythm. Make full-length slices from the bladder neck to the veru. Do not waste time cauterizing bleeding sites unless vision is seriously impaired or the capsule is reached. The resection does not have to be perfect; just good enough. Try to leave some extra time at the end to search for bleeders. Using video can slow you down. Consider using just direct vision. The position of the surgeon's head will prevent inadvertent distal repositioning of the scope. It is a good idea to learn how to do the TURP both with video guidance and direct vision in case there is a problem with the video in the middle of the case.

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- End of the Surgery - Finishing it Up
retrieved_docs.content: Unless very skilled and experienced, hold the resectoscope very still when cutting. Experts can move the cutting loop and the resectoscope at the same time, but this is an acquired skill and much more challenging to master with video surgery compared to the old-fashioned direct vision.

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- The Middle - Performing the Resection
retrieved_docs.content: Do not cauterize bleeding vessels until the surgical capsule is reached unless they interfere with vision. Otherwise, the same vessel will be cauterized multiple times instead of just once, wasting time.

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- The Beginning - Getting Started
retrieved_docs.content: The table height should be adjusted to a comfortable position for the surgeon.

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- The Beginning - Getting Started
retrieved_docs.content: Confirm that the cutting wire loops fit perfectly into the resectoscope sheath without gaps. Otherwise, resection may be incomplete, leaving tags on tissue that interfere with vision and require a second cut that wastes valuable time.

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- The Beginning - Getting Started
retrieved_docs.content: A spare set of instruments, including extra cutting loops, connectors, and a backup electrosurgical generator, should be immediately available if necessary.

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- Discussion
retrieved_docs.content: Transurethral resection of the prostate is an art that requires substantial practice to master. Since urologists are performing fewer of these procedures now than in the past, it is more difficult for most to develop very high skill levels.

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- Discussion
retrieved_docs.content: "The actual technique is probably not as important as planning it carefully and executing it precisely so that the operation is complete and the surgeon remains oriented throughout the procedure." Winston Mebust

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- Discussion
retrieved_docs.content: There are also some disadvantages. There are essentially three distinct, separate stages to the surgery, and it would be difficult to quickly stop the procedure until all the surgical steps are completed. The initial groove made at the roof is done without any visual landmarks in an area where the prostate is the thinnest. Any early bleeding or perforation will significantly increase the complication rate and severity. The lateral lobes can easily overcrowd the prostatic fossa, hide bleeding sites, and not leave enough room for easy resection due to interference with the mobility of the resectoscope. Any early bleeding can be difficult to see or control with bulky lateral lobe tissue in the way.

retrieved_docs.title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- Discussion
retrieved_docs.content: The Milner technique offers several advantages over the Nesbit procedure. Immediate attention is given to the bulky lateral lobe tissue, removing it quickly, offering improved vision and better irrigation with increased resectoscope mobility. One entire side is completed before moving to the opposite side, which provides the opportunity to stop the procedure midway if necessary. Partially resected lateral lobes tend to bleed more than if only incompletely resected, as with the Nesbit technique. If the initial groove is made incorrectly or too deeply, there is minimal harm if it is done at the extreme lateral position of 9 or 3 o'clock, where the tissue is thickest, and any inadvertent capsular perforation will have a minimal clinical effect. The full-length prostatic groove provides its own distal and proximal landmarks, which then only have to be extended by rotating the resectoscope and repeating the linear resection. Leaving the anterior prostatic tissue for last minimizes the risk of any accidental perforation in this risky area.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 4 -- Community 2879
retrieved_docs.content: Open prostatectomy is classified as an operation by approach, indicating that it is a surgical procedure performed through a direct, open incision to access the prostate. As a member of the broader category of operations by approach, open prostatectomy exemplifies surgeries that rely on an open technique rather than minimally invasive or endoscopic methods, aiding clinicians in selecting appropriate surgical strategies and informing patients about the nature of the procedure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1523
retrieved_docs.content: Operations by approach encompasses open prostatectomy, indicating that open prostatectomy is a specific surgical approach classified under Operations by approach. Both open prostatectomy and Transrectal biopsy of prostate are linked to عمليات البروستاتا (prostate operations), showing they belong to the broader category of prostate surgeries. Transrectal biopsy of prostate is also categorized as a diagnostic procedure for the male genital system, reflecting its role in evaluating prostate pathology. Clinically, عمليات البروستاتا are associated with خلل وظيفي في الانتصاب (erectile dysfunction), highlighting a potential adverse effect of prostate surgeries on sexual function. Understanding these relationships aids clinicians in selecting appropriate surgical approaches, recognizing diagnostic options, and counseling patients about possible functional outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1516
retrieved_docs.content: Operations by approach is a broad category that includes open prostatectomy as a specific surgical method, indicating that open prostatectomy is classified under this approach-based grouping. Both open prostatectomy and transrectal biopsy of prostate are further placed within the larger Arabic‑language category عمليات البروستاتا, which denotes prostate operations in general. This hierarchical organization shows that open prostatectomy represents an invasive, open surgical technique for treating prostate conditions, while transrectal biopsy of prostate is a minimally invasive diagnostic procedure, yet both are considered types of prostate operations. Recognizing these relationships helps clinicians categorize prostate interventions by their procedural approach and overall procedural family, facilitating clearer communication and more systematic patient care planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2699
retrieved_docs.content: Open prostatectomy is encompassed within the broader category of عمليات البروستاتا (prostate surgeries). Clinically, خلل وظيفي في الانتصاب (erectile dysfunction) is associated with عمليات البروستاتا, indicating that patients undergoing prostate surgeries, including open prostatectomy, may experience erectile dysfunction as a potential complication. Recognizing this relationship is important for pre‑operative counseling, risk assessment, and postoperative management to optimize patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 623
retrieved_docs.content: Transrectal biopsy of prostate is classified within عمليات البروستاتا, the broader category of prostate procedures. Clinical evidence shows that عمليات البروستاتا are associated with خلل وظيفي في الانتصاب, indicating that patients who undergo prostate interventions—including transrectal biopsy—may be at risk for erectile dysfunction. Recognizing this link is important for informing patients, monitoring postoperative outcomes, and guiding appropriate management strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2866
retrieved_docs.content: Transurethral resection of prostate (TURP) is a surgical procedure that is clinically associated with both فرط تنسج بروستاتي حميد (benign prostatic hyperplasia) and تضخم بروستاتي (prostatic enlargement). These relationships reflect the use of TURP as a therapeutic intervention to relieve urinary obstruction and other symptoms caused by the benign overgrowth of prostate tissue. Recognizing this connection is important for clinicians when selecting appropriate management strategies for patients presenting with prostatic hyperplasia or enlargement.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2156
retrieved_docs.content: "Operations by approach" encompasses "open prostatectomy" as a specific surgical technique classified by its approach. Both "open prostatectomy" and "Transrectal biopsy of prostate" are included within the broader category of "عمليات البروستاتا" (prostate operations). Clinical evidence shows that "عمليات البروستاتا" are associated with "خلل وظيفي في الانتصاب" (erectile dysfunction). Recognizing that open prostatectomy is defined by its operative approach and that transrectal biopsy and open prostatectomy are types of prostate operations aids clinicians in anticipating and managing the potential functional complication of erectile dysfunction in patients undergoing these procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 2 -- Community 641
retrieved_docs.content: Prostatolithotomy, prostatotomy with calculus removal, and transurethral prostate incision are all classified as “other or therapeutic procedures; male genital,” and each of these interventions is performed directly on the prostate. The prostate itself is identified as the anatomic site associated with metastatic prostate cancer and serves as the location for prostatic disorders. Other male genital disorders are likewise situated in the prostate, are considered a subset of prostatic disorders, and are linked to screening activities. Together, these relationships highlight the prostate as a central focus for both disease processes—such as metastatic cancer and various prostatic disorders—and for therapeutic procedures aimed at treating conditions within this organ.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 3 -- Community 1517
retrieved_docs.content: Transrectal biopsy of prostate and open prostatectomy are both classified under the broader category of عمليات البروستاتا (prostate procedures). As prostate procedures, they represent distinct interventions used in the evaluation and management of prostate disease: the transrectal biopsy of prostate is a diagnostic technique that obtains tissue samples for histologic assessment, while open prostatectomy is a surgical approach that removes prostate tissue to treat conditions such as benign prostatic hyperplasia or localized prostate cancer. Recognizing that both procedures belong to the same overarching group of prostate operations helps clinicians select appropriate diagnostic or therapeutic strategies within the context of prostate health care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 4 -- Community 2777
retrieved_docs.content: Prostate surgeries (عمليات البروستاتا) are clinically associated with erectile dysfunction (خلل وظيفي في الانتصاب), indicating that patients undergoing prostate operations may experience erectile dysfunction as a potential complication, which is important for pre‑operative counseling and postoperative management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 1 -- Community 162
retrieved_docs.content: the primary site of prostatic disorder and related male genital diseases. Common pathologies include benign prostatic hyperplasia (فرط تنسج بروستاتي حميد), prostatic enlargement (تضخم بروستاتي), and prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا, سرطان البروستاتا النَّقيلي). Therapeutic strategies comprise transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP) for BPH and related enlargement, open prostatectomy (which may involve partial removal of adjacent structures such as the seminal vesicles), and transurethral prostate incision. Urinary tract procedures (عملية في السبيل البولي) frequently employ an indwelling catheter as a parent entity, and systematic screening (التحصّي) of male genital disorders emphasizes early detection and monitoring of prostate and related conditions, illustrating the integrated anatomy, pathology, and treatment framework in male genitourinary care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 1674
retrieved_docs.content: Open prostatectomy is a specific surgical technique that falls under the broader category of عمليات البروستاتا (prostate surgeries). It is employed as a treatment modality for أورام البروستاتا الخبيثة (SMQ) (malignant prostate tumors), indicating that this operative approach is directly related to the management of prostate cancer within the overall spectrum of prostate surgical interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 1 -- Community 227
retrieved_docs.content: include Prostatolithotomy, Prostatotomy and calculus removal, categorized as other therapeutic procedures; male genital. These procedures, together with transurethral incision of the prostate (شق البروستاتا عبر الإحليل), are performed on the prostate, which serves as the direct procedural site. Diagnostic procedures; male genital involve partial excision of prostate tissue for histologic evaluation. Transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP) is a key therapeutic and diagnostic intervention performed on the prostate. TURP is clinically associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia and prostate enlargement (تضخم بروستاتي), providing relief of obstructive urinary symptoms, and is also linked to prostate cancer and metastatic prostate cancer, where it can aid in tumor diagnosis and symptom palliation. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 2639
retrieved_docs.content: Transrectal biopsy of prostate is classified within the broader category of prostate procedures (عمليات البروستاتا), representing a specific type of operation performed on the prostate gland.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 2 -- Community 719
retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary symptoms and ill‑defined conditions constitute a broad clinical category that encompasses specific bladder disorder (اضطراب في المثانة). This category frequently co‑occurs with urinary stone disease (حصاة حالبية) and with benign prostatic hyperplasia (فرط تنسج بروستاتي حميد), and it is clinically associated with prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا). Transurethral resection of prostate (TURP) is a surgical intervention that is clinically linked to benign prostatic hyperplasia (فرط تنسج بروستاتي حميد) as well as to general prostate enlargement (تضخم بروستاتي). TURP is also associated with prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا), reflecting its role in the diagnosis and management of malignant prostate disease. The interconnections among genitourinary symptoms, bladder disorder, urinary stones, benign and malignant prostate conditions, and the TURP procedure underscore the importance of integrated assessment and treatment strategies in urologic patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 1 -- Community 173
retrieved_docs.content: (inflammatory conditions of male genital organs). Procedures aimed at genitourinary incontinence are related to the urinary system and are considered a form of bladder treatment (علاج المثانة), underscoring their role in managing bladder function and continence. Cosmetic surgery (جراحة التجميل) is also linked to bladder treatment, highlighting possible overlap between aesthetic and urological interventions. The prostate (prostata) is part of the urinary system and serves as the anatomical site for multiple disorders such as اضطراب بروستاتي, التهاب البروستاتا, benign prostatic hyperplasia, prostate enlargement, prostate cancer, and metastatic prostate cancer. These disorders are grouped within other male genital disorders (other male genital disorders) and are associated with preventive measures (التحصّي).
